essence: Installs and maintains passive fire‑protection systems that slow or prevent the spread of fire and smoke within buildings.

identity.title: Passive Fire Protection Technician
identity.summary: Installs fire‑stopping, cavity barriers, fire‑resistant boards, coatings, and structural protection to ensure buildings meet fire‑safety standards.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in complex commercial or high‑rise passive‑fire systems can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Knowledge of fire‑stopping materials and methods
Ability to read construction drawings and specifications
Understanding of building regulations and fire‑resistance standards

lifestyle.description: Passive fire‑protection technicians work on construction sites, refurbishments, and commercial buildings installing fire‑stopping, cavity barriers, structural protection, and fire‑resistant materials. The role suits individuals who enjoy practical work, construction environments, and contributing to life‑safety compliance.

habits: Follows strict safety and compliance standards
Keeps detailed photographic and written records
Communicates clearly with site managers and inspectors
Checks materials and installation quality carefully
